p.strong: More frames can cause MORE page faults with FIFO

ul.li: At step 7, both cases fault on page 5
4-frames must fault again to reload page 1

div.p: Key Insight: FIFO is NOT a stack algorithm. Pages in memory with N+1 frames are NOT always a superset of pages with N frames. This violates the inclusion property.
The Fix: LRU and Optimal are stack algorithms. They are IMMUNE to Bélády's anomaly — more frames never increases faults.

g.text: ❌ ANOMALY: More faults with more frames

text.tspan: // FIFO only considers LOAD ORDER, not ACCESS PATTERN
// With 3 frames: page 1 evicted just BEFORE being reused
// With 4 frames: page 1 stays LONGER, evicted just BEFORE reuse
// The extra frame changes eviction TIMING, not just capacity
// FIFO only considers LOAD ORDER, not ACCESS PATTERN
// With 3 frames: page 1 evicted just BEFORE being reused
// With 4 frames: page 1 stays LONGER, evicted just BEFORE reuse
// The extra frame changes eviction TIMING, not just capacity
